subject: fix(types): use Supabase .returns<T>() for join type instead of cast hack
body:
Replace the `as unknown[]` cast with Supabase's `.returns<FuelPriceRow[]>()`
API method. This is the intended way to type queries where Supabase's
select-string parser infers the wrong shape (FK joins parsed as arrays
when they're actually single objects at runtime).

Runtime validation filter kept as a safety net for schema drift.

Tags: typescript, supabase, types
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: codebase-review
